expectations.evidence: profiles.toml lines 10-12: `[default.env]` section contains both `AWS_ACCESS_KEY_ID = "{{ .Env.AWS_ACCESS_KEY_ID }}"` and `AWS_SECRET_ACCESS_KEY = "{{ .Env.AWS_SECRET_ACCESS_KEY }}"`

expectations.text: 일요일 오전 3시 schedule이 설정됨 (Sun 03:00 또는 유사 표현)
expectations.evidence: profiles.toml line 28: `schedule = "Sun *-*-* 03:00:00"` — systemd calendar format for every Sunday at 03:00

expectations.text: run-after에 healthchecks.io URL이 포함된 curl 명령어가 있음
expectations.evidence: profiles.toml lines 18-20: `run-after = ["curl -fsS --retry 3 https://hc-ping.com/${HC_UUID}"]` — uses hc-ping.com which is the healthchecks.io ping domain

expectations.text: run-after-fail에 healthchecks.io fail URL이 포함된 curl 명령어가 있음
expectations.evidence: profiles.toml lines 23-25: `run-after-fail = ["curl -fsS --retry 3 https://hc-ping.com/${HC_UUID}/fail"]` — uses the /fail endpoint of hc-ping.com

The profiles.toml file uses correct TOML syntax throughout: table headers in brackets, key = value pairs with proper quoting, and valid array literals. The nested table structure ([default], [default.env], [default.backup]) is valid TOML.

claims.claim: scheduler = "systemd" is set at the global level
AWS credentials are read from environment variables via template syntax
claims.evidence: profiles.toml uses `{{ .Env.AWS_ACCESS_KEY_ID }}` and `{{ .Env.AWS_SECRET_ACCESS_KEY }}` which is resticprofile Go-template syntax for reading host environment variables at runtime

claims.claim: The hc-ping.com domain is the healthchecks.io ping endpoint
claims.evidence: hc-ping.com is the official ping domain used by healthchecks.io; the /fail suffix signals a failure ping

claims.claim: The configuration uses a password-file for repository encryption
claims.evidence: profiles.toml line 7: `password-file = "/etc/resticprofile/password"` — this is a placeholder path that must be created before use

suggestions.reason: The assertion passes whether the env section contains literal placeholder strings (e.g., 'YOUR_KEY_HERE') or proper environment-variable references. A stronger check would verify that the values are not hardcoded secrets and that some form of indirection (template syntax, env var reference, or empty string with instructions) is used.

No assertion checks that run-after and run-after-fail are placed inside the [default.backup] section (or another backup profile section) rather than at the top-level profile. resticprofile only honours these hooks when they are scoped to a backup command block. The generated config places them correctly, but a wrong placement would pass all current assertions.

eval_feedback.overall: Assertions cover all required fields and generally reflect meaningful requirements. The two suggestions above address cases where a superficially correct output could pass without being functionally correct.
